title: viola mandshurica var. ikedaeana
intro: Viola mandshurica var. ikedaeana, also known as Korean violet, is a perennial plant that belongs to the violet family. It is traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing respiratory issues, as well as possessing immunomodulatory and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Viola mandshurica var. ikedaeana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional remedies like jams and wines, to harness its medicinal properties.
